fields.name: Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number that is assigned to the entry.

fields.name: Item No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the value from the same field on the physical inventory tracking line.

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location of the traced item.

fields.name: Quantity (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the item that has been reserved in the entry.

fields.name: Reservation Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of the reservation.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies a description of the record.

fields.name: Creation Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the traced record was created.

fields.name: Transferred from Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a value when the order tracking entry is for the quantity that remains on a document line after a partial posting.

fields.name: Source Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies for which source type the reservation entry is related to.

fields.name: Source Subtype
fields.tooltip: Specifies which source subtype the reservation entry is related to.

fields.name: Source ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies which source ID the reservation entry is related to.

fields.name: Source Batch Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the journal batch name if the reservation entry is related to a journal or requisition line.

fields.name: Source Prod. Order Line
fields.tooltip: Contains the production order line, which is copied field from the Line No. field if the reservation entry is related to a production order line.

fields.name: Source Ref. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a reference number for the line, which the reservation entry is related to.

fields.name: Item Ledger Entry No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies item tracking numbers used in connection with combined shipments/return receipts.
The field contains a reference to the associated item ledger entry.

fields.name: Expected Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date you expect the items to be available in your warehouse. If you leave the field blank, it will be calculated as follows: Planned Receipt Date + Safety Lead Time + Inbound Warehouse Handling Time = Expected Receipt Date.

fields.name: Shipment Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when items on the document are shipped or were shipped. A shipment date is usually calculated from a requested delivery date plus lead time.

fields.name: Serial No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the serial number of the item that is being handled on the document line.

fields.name: Created By
fields.tooltip: Specifies the user who created the traced record.

fields.name: Changed By
fields.tooltip: Contains the ID of the user who changed the reservation.

fields.name: Positive
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the difference is positive.

fields.name: Qty. per Unit of Measure
fields.tooltip: Specifies how many of the base unit of measure are contained in one unit of the item.

fields.name: Quantity
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of the record.

fields.name: Action Message Adjustment
fields.tooltip:
Shows the change in the supply side of the order tracking that results when you accept the related action messages.
A value appears here only when the facilities for both order tracking and action messages are active. The value is calculated on the basis of the data in the Action Message Entry table.

fields.name: Binding
fields.tooltip:
Controls supply orders that are bound to a specific demand, such as production orders that are created directly from a sales order.
The field displays Order-to-Order when the entry is tied specifically to a demand or a supply.
The field is left blank if the entry is not bound to any specific demand or supply.

fields.name: Suppressed Action Msg.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally.

fields.name: Planning Flexibility
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether the supply represented by the reservation line is considered by the planning system when calculating action messages.
If the field contains the option Unlimited, then the planning system includes the line when calculating action messages.
If the field contains the option None, then the line is firm and unchangeable, and the planning system does not include the line when calculating action messages.

fields.name: Appl.-to Item Entry
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the item ledger entry that the reserved entry was applied to.

fields.name: Warranty Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last day of the serial/lot number's warranty.

fields.name: Expiration Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the expiration date of the lot or serial number on the item tracking line.

fields.name: Qty. to Handle (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity of item, in the base unit of measure, to be handled in a warehouse activity.

fields.name: Qty. to Invoice (Base)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quantity, in the base unit of measure, that remains to be invoiced. It is calculated as Quantity - Qty. Invoiced.

fields.name: Quantity Invoiced (Base)
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the quantity of serial/lot numbers that is invoiced with the associated document line expressed in base units of measure.
The field is displayed in the Item Tracking Lines window.

fields.name: New Serial No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a new serial number that replaces the serial number in the Serial No. field when you post the item reclassification journal.

fields.name: New Lot No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies a new lot number that replaces the lot number in the Lot No. field when you post the item reclassification journal.

fields.name: Disallow Cancellation
fields.tooltip:
Indicates that the reservation entry represents the link between a sales order line and an assembly order.
You cannot delete this reservation because it is required to maintain the synchronization that occurs when an item is assembled to order.

fields.name: Lot No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the lot number of the item that is being handled on the document line.

fields.name: Variant Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the variant of the item on the line.

fields.name: Appl.-from Item Entry
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. It contains the entry number of the item ledger entry that the program will use to retrieve the cost for this line, provided that this line is an inbound transaction. When you post the transaction, the program uses the unit cost from the applied-from outbound item ledger entry as the cost for the new inbound item ledger entry.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether this item ledger entry has been posted as a corrective entry.

fields.name: New Expiration Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a new expiration date.
In this field, you can enter a new expiration date to take the place of the expiration date currently associated with the lot or serial number. The expiration date is the date after which the goods will no longer be usable.
If you are reclassifying a lot to the same lot number but with a different expiration date, you must reclassify the entire lot, using one item reclassification journal line.
If you are reclassifying more than one lot to one new lot number, meaning that you are merging more than one lot into one new lot, you must enter the same new expiration date for all the lots.

fields.name: Item Tracking
fields.tooltip:
This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use. The purpose of the field is to make it easier to filter the entries with item tracking.
The field is updated automatically. It specifies whether the field carries item tracking and if so, whether it carries a lot number, a serial number, or both a lot and serial number.

fields.name: Package No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the package number of the item that is being handled on the document line.
